title: Beehiiv vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Substack for newsletters. See pricing, features, ratings, and which platform suits your needs best.
h1: Beehiiv vs Substack: Complete Comparison for Newsletter Creators
intro: Both Beehiiv and Substack are popular newsletter platforms, but they serve different creator needs. Beehiiv focuses on growth tools and multiple monetisation streams, while Substack emphasizes direct writer-to-reader relationships with built-in audience discovery. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right platform for your newsletter business.
choose_a_if: You want multiple revenue streams including ads, sponsorships, and referral programs alongside paid subscriptions
You're building a media business or operating multiple newsletters and need advanced segmentation and analytics
You prefer transparent pricing with no revenue share, keeping 100% of your subscription income
choose_b_if: You want to start publishing immediately with no subscriber limits on the free plan
You prefer a revenue-share model and benefit from Substack's built-in discovery network and audience recommendations
You're a solo writer focused on direct reader relationships and podcast hosting interests you

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your goals. Beehiiv excels for growth-focused publishers needing multiple monetisation options and advanced analytics, earning higher user satisfaction ratings (4.7 on G2 vs 4.0). Substack works better for solo writers wanting unlimited free subscribers and built-in audience discovery without platform fees.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Substack?
faq.a: Substack's revenue-share model (10% commission) is cheaper if you're just starting and keeping costs low. However, Beehiiv's fixed pricing ($39+/month) becomes better value as you scale, since Substack takes a permanent percentage cut. For most creators under 1,000 paid subscribers, Substack costs less.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Substack and import it into Beehiiv, though the process requires manual CSV export. Your email content and archive won't transfer automatically, so plan for setup time. Both platforms support standard email list migration through their admin dashboards.
